Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a temporal profile model with several pairs of activities and their average time intervals and standard deviations We are to identify anomalies hypothesize reasons and propose verification SQL queries Step 1 Identify anomalies in the temporal profile model Looking at the provided temporal profile R P 90000 3600 Average 25 hours STDEV 1 hour very low STDEV for a 25hour average P N 604800 172800 Average 7 days STDEV 2 days long and variable A C 7200 3600 Average 2 hours STDEV 1 hour closing soon after assignment E N 300 60 Average 5 minutes STDEV 1 minute very quick from evaluation to notification Also note that we have an expected process R A E P N C Some pairs are not in the expected sequence but the model includes them We are to look for unusual times

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 2 Generate hypotheses For each anomaly 1 R P Average 25 hours with a very low standard deviation 1 hour This could be due to A fixed policy that approves claims exactly 25 hours after receipt without considering the evaluation step Possibly a system that automatically approves after a fixed time or a batch process that runs every 25 hours 2 P N Average 7 days with a high standard deviation 2 days This could be due to A backlog in the notification system where notifications are sent in batches every few days Some claims requiring additional manual steps before notification leading to variable delays 3 A C Average 2 hours from assignment to close This could be due to Some claims being closed immediately after assignment without proper evaluation possibly due to fraudulent or invalid claims that are quickly rejected A quick resolution for certain types of claims that require little evaluation 4 E N Average 5 minutes from evaluation to notification This could be due to An automated notification system that sends notifications immediately after evaluation Or if evaluation is done by the adjuster and they have a habit of immediately notifying this might be too quick and could indicate that the evaluation is not thorough

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 3 Propose verification approaches using SQL queries We will write queries to check the actual time differences between these activities in the claim_events table Note The claim_events table has claim_id activity and timestamp We can compute the time between two activities for each claim by joining the table on itself for the same claim_id and different activities Well write queries for each of the anomalies we identified Lets start with the R P anomaly We want to find claims where the time between R and P is very close to 90000 seconds 25 hours with a low variance However note that the model says average 25 hours with a low STDEV So we can look for claims where the time between R and P is within say 1 hour of 25 hours ie between 86400 and 93600 seconds and see if there are many Alternatively we can compute the actual average and STDEV in the database and compare Since we are to propose verification queries we can write Query 1 Check the distribution of time between R and P for all claims Query 1 Compute the average and standard deviation of time between R and P Query 2 Check if the anomaly is present in all claim types or only in some Now for P N with long delays Query 3 Compute the average and standard deviation of time between P and N and break down by claim_type We might also want to check if there are claims that skip the evaluation step E or have an unusual sequence Query 4 Check for claims that are assigned A and then closed C without any evaluation E in between For the A C anomaly check if the adjusters involved are in a particular region or specialization Query 5 For the A C anomaly check if the adjusters involved are in a particular region or specialization For E N with very short time Query 6 Check the claims where the time between E and N is less than say 10 minutes 600 seconds and see if they are associated with particular adjusters or claim types We can also check if there is a pattern of skipping the approval step P in these cases Query 7 Check if claims that go from E to N quickly also have the P activity and if so in what order Note The above queries are written for PostgreSQL and use EXTRACT EPOCH FROM to get seconds We must be cautious of the fact that the same claim might have multiple events of the same type The schema doesnt specify but typically each activity is recorded once per claim However the query for A C with a left join to E is designed to check for the absence of E in between

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We have now proposed several queries to verify the anomalies Lets summarize We identified four anomalies and for each we proposed queries to check the actual data and break down by claim type adjuster etc
